kept.text:
🚨AMD's Second Act: From GPU Challenger to AI Infrastructure Duopoly

AMD reported Q1 2026 results with revenue of $10.25B (+38% YoY), ahead of the $9.84B consensus; Non-GAAP EPS of $1.37 (+43% YoY), also beating expectations. GAAP net income came in at $1.38B (+95% YoY); Non-GAAP net income reached $2.27B (+45% YoY), with Non-GAAP gross margin at 55%. Free cash flow hit a record $2.6B for the quarter, more than tripling year-over-year.
On the surface, the financials were a modest beat across the board — but AMD stock surged more than 18% in after-hours trading, briefly topping $410. The numbers alone don't explain the move. What does: CEO Lisa Su's forward guidance. Su stated that the server CPU TAM will double to $120B by 2030, that annual data center AI revenue is on track to reach "tens of billions of dollars," and reaffirmed a long-term Non-GAAP EPS target of over $20.

⚡️Core Theme: Data Center Takes the Wheel
Data Center revenue reached $5.78B, up 57% YoY, crossing the halfway mark of total company revenue and becoming the primary driver of both top-line and earnings growth — powered by the dual engine of EPYC server CPUs and Instinct GPUs.
The market's historical read on AMD's AI thesis was straightforward: can MI300/MI350/MI450 take share from NVIDIA? What this quarter's management commentary reframes is that agentic AI and inference workloads are driving a significant uplift in CPU demand as well. AI clusters don't just need GPUs for training and inference — they require substantial CPU capacity for orchestration, data preprocessing, head node management, and parallel task scheduling. AMD's advantage is now expanding from a single-point GPU play into a compound architecture: EPYC + Instinct + Helios, together.
Critically, CEO Lisa Su raised AMD's server CPU TAM outlook significantly: the addressable market is now expected to grow at over 35% annually, reaching more than $120B by 2030 — effectively doubling the prior forecast of ~18% CAGR and a ~$60B TAM.

🌞Product Pipeline: MI450 / Helios Enter the Visible Order Cycle
On the AI accelerator front, AMD confirmed that MI450 series GPUs have begun sampling with lead customers, and Helios rack-scale AI systems remain on track for production shipments in H2 2026. Su noted that customer demand forecasts for MI450 and Helios have already exceeded AMD's original 2027 plans, with new customers now in discussions for large-scale deployments — including additional multi-gigawatt opportunities.
More significantly, AMD raised its confidence in 2027 data center AI revenue: management expressed conviction in achieving tens of billions of dollars in annual data center AI revenue in 2027, ahead of the prior long-term target of greater than 80% CAGR.
On the hyperscaler side, the order book is becoming concrete: OpenAI and Meta have each committed to deploying 6GW of Instinct compute; Oracle plans to launch the world's first publicly available AI supercluster powered by 50,000 MI450 GPUs in Q3. Taken together, these three commitments are moving AMD's status as "AI compute's second source" from narrative to reality.
Q2 Outlook: Above Expectations, Data Center Continues to Accelerate
AMD guided Q2 revenue to approximately $11.2B (±$300M), meaningfully above the $10.5B consensus, representing roughly 46% growth YoY and 9% sequentially. Non-GAAP gross margin is guided at approximately 56%. Server CPU is expected to grow more than 70% for the full year, with both data center AI and server businesses projected to deliver double-digit sequential growth.

📈Bottom Line
This quarter isn't just another beat. The on-track delivery of MI450 and Helios has moved AMD from "potential NVIDIA alternative" to "confirmed co-anchor of AI infrastructure."
The after-hours surge to above $410 implies roughly 30x that $20 long-term EPS target — the market is pricing it in today. Notably, AMD had gone virtually nowhere over the prior three sessions, with tonight's after-hours move catching it up to Intel's recent gains. The capital rotation story isn't complicated: last Friday, Western Digital's blowout earnings ignited a fresh AI hardware rally, with funds rotating out of NVIDIA into memory and CPU names — Western Digital and Micron gained 28% and 24% respectively over three sessions, Intel added 15%, and AMD's earnings tonight became the final piece of that rotation trade.
Looking further out, the key variables are whether MI450 and Helios ship on schedule, whether the Meta and OpenAI deployments convert into durable multi-year order flow, and whether EPYC can continue capturing share as AI-driven CPU demand structurally expands.

kept.text:
市場傳出，主因是NVIDIA下一代Rubin GPU均熱片設計出現重大變更，原規劃的雙片式（雙lid＋stiffener＋鍍金）改回單片式，導致單價從150美元大幅下滑至約50美元，降幅達66%，嚴重衝擊市場對健策AI業務高成長的預期。

據供應鏈資訊，健策自今年3月已開始出貨Rubin新版雙lid均熱片，單價較前一代Blackwell的單片式提升約5倍，原被視為2026-2027年業績爆發的重要動能。然而NVIDIA本週要求供應鏈調整，自5月起改採單片式設計。變更主因是Rubin GPU功耗高達1800W-2300W，原採用液態金屬TIM2方案因組裝時易外溢，改回石墨材料，連帶取消水冷板鍍金與雙lid需求。

供應鏈估計，以Rubin 200萬顆出貨目標計算，此變更將影響健策2026年約60億元營收；若2027年出貨達400-450萬顆，影響金額更高達130億元，市場對健策2026、2027年營收成長預估分別下修約15%與20%。法人已將第二季營收季增幅自30%下修至10%左右。

不過，設計調整急迫下，Blackwell GPU出貨可能短期增加形成替代效應。長期來看，健策仍有望受惠CPO（Spectrum 5、Quantum 5）stiffener、AMD MI450均熱片、以及英特爾Oak Stream CPU socket等新業務。

https://t.co/RGRKwLEsTO

kept.text:
AMD的服务器CPU会因为agentic AI变得更重要，过去一个多月我们一直在讲。高盛昨天终于跟上来了，把AMD从Neutral直接升到Buy，目标价从$240调到$450。sell-side对CPU叙事的认知一直是滞后X上的讨论的。

高盛的逻辑：agentic系统会大幅增加CPU的工作负载。它给出的数据是GPU和CPU的attach rate从8:1降到了2:1，行业在向CPU-only机架加GPU/ASIC机架的混合架构演进，x86在企业端的兼容性优势让AMD成为主要受益者。它预计到2030年agentic相关token将占总token量的90%，全球token消耗量将是当前产能的24倍。由此它预测AMD服务器CPU收入2027/28年到$211亿和$274亿，比街面高24%和38%。

GPU端主要靠Meta的6GW部署撑量（含1.6亿股AMD股票的warrant），微软和OpenAI都给了保守假设，建模AMD GPU出货量2026/27年同比增长66%/98%，数据中心GPU收入2027/28年$425亿/$563亿，高于街面28%/26%。整体来看，高盛预计AMD数据中心收入占比将从2024年的49%提升到2027年的77%，传统业务（PC、Gaming、Embedded）合计不到25%，周期性下行已被市场消化。目标价$450对应30x P/E（从28x上调），标准化EPS从$8.50上调到$15。

此外，高盛在这份报告里明确说，它仍然更看好Broadcom和Nvidia。理由是这两家估值相对不高，上行空间靠盈利上修和估值重估驱动，而AMD当前的估值倍数已经部分反映了CPU增长改善的预期。升级了AMD，但排序上还是把它放在NVDA和AVGO后面，这本身就说明问题。

高盛2027/28年的EPS预测是$13.50/$18.00，比街面高约20%，这个差值会在未来几个季度的guidance里逐步验证。1Q26 AMD已经交出了不错的成绩单，收入$102.5亿同比增长38%，毛利率55.4%均超预期。2Q26指引更强，$112亿收入中位数比街面高6%，毛利率指引56%。

profile_bio.description:
Working at RadixArk, SGLang Core Contributor | Ex-Intern at Nvidia, Baidu

Linkedin: https://t.co/zWu1LAMuLR

quoted_tweet.text:
Today, we are thrilled to officially launch RadixArk with $100M in Seed funding at a $400M valuation. The round was led by @Accel and co-led by @sparkcapital.

RadixArk exists to make frontier AI infrastructure open and accessible to everyone. Today, the systems behind the most capable AI models are concentrated in a small number of companies. As a result, most AI teams are forced to rebuild training and inference stacks from scratch, duplicating the same infrastructure work instead of focusing on new models, products, and ideas.

RadixArk was founded to change that. We are building an AI platform that makes it easier for teams to train and serve the best models at scale.

RadixArk comes from the open-source community. We started with SGLang, where many of us are core developers and maintainers, and expanded our work to Miles for large-scale RL and post-training. We will continue contributing to both projects and working with the community to make them the strongest open-source infrastructure foundations for frontier AI.

We would like to thank our long-term partners, contributors, and the broader SGLang community for believing in this mission. We're also grateful to @Accel and @sparkcapital, NVentures (Venture capital arm of @nvidia), Salience Capital, A&E Investment, @HOFCapital, @walden_catalyst, @AMD, LDVP, WTT Fubon Family, @MediaTek, Vocal Ventures, @Sky9Capital and our angel investors @ibab, @LipBuTan1, Hock Tan, @johnschulman2, @soumithchintala, @lilianweng, @oliveur, @Thom_Wolf, @LiamFedus, @robertnishihara, @ericzelikman, @OfficialLoganK, and @multiply_matrix among others.

Thanks for the exclusive interview with @MeghanBobrowsky at @WSJ about our vision.

profile_bio.description: A Trend Following Theme Investor with a learning-driven approach to trading. Process over Outcome, Context over Patterns, Stocks over Setups, EC over Ego.

kept.text:
字节跳动今年GPU采购预算从1600亿上调至2300亿，CPU预算从700亿增至1000亿元。海外采购约1200亿（NVIDIA 65%、AMD 30%、Broadcom 5%），国内采购核心分三块：寒武纪250-300亿、华为昇腾220-230亿、自研芯片170-190亿。明年CAPEX可能再翻倍，GPU预算或达5000亿以上。

字节通过按照1:4的比例搭配采购国产芯片，换取NVIDIA H200和5090的配额，额外带动200多亿英伟达预算。

国产芯片全线满产满销，产能而非需求是瓶颈。自研卡走三星6nm代工，单卡成本3600-3700美元，芯原拿300-400美元设计服务利润。腾讯CAPEX激进到2800亿，给昆仑芯下了30万张卡的大单。2027年寒武纪订单至少500亿起。

最受益标的排序
第一梯队是寒武纪，确定性最高。690芯片今年量产全部被字节吃下，250-300亿保底预算，2027年翻倍至500亿以上，订单和收入完全不用担心，唯一变量是上游产能。

第二梯队是芯原股份，隐藏赢家。字节自研卡每张给芯原300-400美元利润，30万张就是近1亿美元增量。腾讯自研芯片也要走芯原到三星的通道，昆仑芯同样选择三星代工。芯原作为"通道公司"在国产自研芯片浪潮中卡位独特。

第三梯队是三星代工产业链受益方。字节、百度昆仑芯、腾讯自研全部押注三星，6nm产能成为稀缺资源。

article.title: 《财报不写的美股发家史》第10期：AI芯片的暗战之王（$AMD）
article.preview_text: 记得是去年的黄金周，在旅游的途中，有个爱玩游戏的朋友突然问我：“你猜PS5的芯片是谁造的？”我随口说是英伟达。他笑着说：“你错了，是AMD。”我不信，赶紧搜了下，才发现AMD居然几乎垄断了游戏机市场。😺

kept.text:
if you want mac portability and you want to learn cuda, the dgx spark is the silent king nobody is talking about.

128gb unified memory in a form factor that fits on a desk corner, full cuda stack, runs nemotron 30b q8 at 56 tok/s on hermes agent, multimodal + tool calls

nobody has written custom kernels for this specific silicon yet. spark has its own architecture (gb10 blackwell, aarch64), the whole ecosystem of model-specific kernel work for 3090 / 4090 / 5090 has not been ported here. that is an openlane for builders who want the territory.                    
                                                           
i expect nvidia to focus on its ecosystem more this year. the hardware is in front of builders, the software needs to catch up to make spark the developer-default for portable ai workstations.

if you have one and you have not written or tested anything model-specific on it yet, you are sitting on the most underexplored consumer AI silicon shipping right now.

kept.text:
到底姜还是老的辣，开启CPU在AI时代重估的源头一定是产业界，一定是走在潮头的英伟达 $NVDA 

以前 GPU 服务器里的 CPU 可以理解为主机 CPU，负责喂数据、跑系统、管理 GPU。

去年9月英伟达发现 AI 不只是 GPU 算矩阵，很多瓶颈在 CPU、内存和数据搬运。

所以它把 CPU 和 GPU 绑得更紧。在把 CPU 拉近 GPU，变成一个高带宽低延迟统一内存的 AI 节点组成部分

从一系列GPU服务器升级就已经开始了CPU和GPU的配比提升到1:2 这部分英伟达内部的GPU服务器的CPU增量肯定是被自己的ARM 自研的CPU吃掉的。

author.name: Tigris 会讲课教授是好老师

profile_bio.description: Fund partner & Professor (CFA), 30 yrs in macro & tech. Longform on AI, semis, cycles. Buy-side mindset: Facts cheap, Interpretation expensive. 中文短评互动拆逻辑.

quoted_tweet.text:
几乎所有人都低估的Intel和英伟达发布会。这波不是主板“换个芯片”，而是把AI高速公路接到x86老城区。

只要互联跑得通、软件跑得稳、OEM跑得快，英伟达的“算力帝国”会更宽，英特尔的“x86江湖”会更深。🇨🇳🇺🇸？

简单讲：看图中间一颗CPU，两边两颗GPU＝一块“超级芯片”，CPU管调度，GPU管重体力活。

现在这块芯片用的是ARM CPU，通过英伟达的NVLink高速互联，最多可把整柜扩到“72块GPU同一个算力域”，你可以理解为72台重卡结成编队。

下一步会发生什么？
把中间那颗CPU换成英特尔的x86。意义不在“换芯片”，

而在换生态：原本只吃得动ARM的场景，现在x86老软件+企业标准都能无缝接上: 数据中心、AI PC、工业/服务机器人，云-边-端一条龙。

很多人忽略的点，发布会上讲得很直白：短期还是台积电代工，毕竟晶圆制造最牛。

想象空间在下一步：如果英特尔晶圆厂达标，出于美国本土供应链安全和地缘风险对冲，从封装到未来和台积电竞争代工，完全有可能。

这就是美国制造业回归的历史事件，和中国台海对抗的最大底气。

quoted_tweet.text:
Blackwell was built for 1T+ parameter frontier intelligence.

On DeepSeek-V4-PRO, the flagship 1.6T-parameter model, NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 delivers 30x better performance per watt than H200 at similar interactivity.

Out of the box, that translates to:
→ 150+ interactivity for low latency agentic systems
→ 20x lower cost per token for long-context agentic workloads

As new optimizations pile on, NVIDIA is leading the charge on  frontier performance  across models built for agentic AI.

profile_bio.description: AI factories for the era of AI reasoning.

kept.text:
While Cerebras lower latency than Nvidia GPU, Nvidia is the most capex and power efficient. 

Cerebras also needs special compilation, topology mapping and memory layout which is technically intensive and is for inference only. Meanwhile Nvidia is a general AI factory and is used for both training and inference. 

I do not have an opinion on Cerebras IPO at this time as it’s hard to project how many people are willing to pay premium for faster inference when Nvidia already provides very fast inference.
